title: Anisotropic magnetism and Kondo-lattice behavior in the frustrated antiferromagnet Ce3MgBi5
authors: Karolina Gornicka, Brenden R. Ortiz, Matthew S. Cook, Heda Zhang, Andrew D. Christianson, Andrew F. May
abstract: We report the synthesis and physical characterization of single-crystalline Ce3MgBi5, a previously unexplored member of the Ce3MPn5 family. This compound crystallizes in the hexagonal P63/mcm structure, featuring an anisotropic Ce sublattice composed of zig-zag chains along the c axis and a distorted kagome-like network in the basal plane. Magnetization measurements reveal antiferromagnetic order below TN approximately 4.2 K, accompanied by strong magnetic anisotropy and multiple field-induced metamagnetic transitions for fields applied perpendicular to [001], leading to a dome-shaped H-T phase diagram. Electrical transport exhibits characteristic signatures of a Ce-based Kondo lattice, including broad resistivity maxima and pronounced field-dependent anomalies in the magnetoresistance and Hall response that track the magnetic phase boundaries. Specific-heat measurements confirm the magnetic transition and show that the full R ln 2 entropy expected for a Ce3+ Kramers doublet is recovered by 20 K, indicating an extended temperature range of magnetic fluctuations consistent with Kondo correlations. Our results establish Ce3MgBi5 as a platform within the Ce3MPn5 family for exploring the interplay of geometric frustration, magnetic anisotropy, and Kondo-lattice physics under applied magnetic fields.
summary: Researchers synthesized and characterized Ce3MgBi5, a new intermetallic compound that exhibits both geometric frustration and Kondo-lattice physics. The material shows a complex magnetic phase diagram and strong coupling between charge transport and magnetic order. This work provides a new platform for studying the competition between RKKY interactions and the Kondo effect in frustrated lattices.
main_problem: Investigating the interplay between geometric frustration, magnetic anisotropy, and Kondo-lattice physics in the newly synthesized Ce3MgBi5 compound.
main_result: The study identifies a Ce-based Kondo-lattice system with antiferromagnetic order below 4.2 K, featuring a complex H-T phase diagram with multiple metamagnetic transitions and strong magnetic anisotropy.
method: Single crystals were synthesized via the Mg-Bi self-flux method and characterized using SQUID magnetometry, PPMS for high-field transport and heat capacity, and X-ray diffraction.
model_or_system: The physical system is Ce3MgBi5, a member of the Ce3MPn5 family with a hexagonal P63/mcm structure featuring a distorted kagome-like Ce sublattice and zig-zag chains along the c-axis.
key_observables: Néel temperature (TN), magnetization (M), electrical resistivity (rho), magnetoresistance (MR), Hall effect, specific heat (Cp), and magnetic entropy (Smag).
important_parameters: TN approx 4.2 K, Weiss temperature (highly anisotropic), Sommerfeld coefficient (gamma approx 199 mJ mol-1 CeK-2), and spin-wave gap (Delta approx 4.96 K).
main_assumptions: The use of La3MgBi5 as a nonmagnetic isostructural reference to subtract phonon background from specific heat measurements.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the crystal structure and Ce sublattice details; Fig 3 shows temperature-dependent resistivity and its derivative; Fig 4 shows temperature-dependent specific heat.
paper_structure: The paper begins with crystal synthesis and structural characterization, followed by magnetic properties and anisotropy, then moves to electrical transport and Hall effect measurements, and concludes with thermodynamic analysis of the Kondo-lattice behavior.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Crystal structure of Ce3MgBi5 highlighting the ar- rangement of Ce (blue), Bi (green), and Mg (orange) atoms. The structure consists of face-sharing MgBi6 octahedra and hypervalent Bi chains running along the c axis. (b) Zig-zag chain of Ce atoms along the c direction with a nearest-neighbor Ce–Ce distance of approximately 3.9 ˚A. (c) Arrangement of Ce atoms within the ab plane, showing two adjacent Ce layers that together form a distorted kagome-like network, with in-plane Ce–Ce bonds of about 5.2 ˚A. (d) X-ray diffraction pattern col- lected from the as-grown facets of a Ce3MgBi5 single crystal. The peak marked with an asterisk (*) originates from residual Bi flux on the...

FIG. 2. (a) The magnetic susceptibility, M/H, as a function of temperature measured in an applied field of 1 kOe for H ⊥[001] and H ∥[001], together with the Curie-Weiss fits (red lines). The vertical dashed line marks the antiferromagnetic transition temperature TN. (b) FC magnetic susceptibility data measured for H ∥[001] under magnetic fields between 5 and 20 kOe (∆H = 5 kOe). (c) M/H vs T for H ⊥[001] under magnetic fields ranging from 1 kOe to 25 kOe, presenting the evolution of the magnetic transition with increasing field. (d) Low-temperature magnetization data for H ⊥[001] at higher fields (∆H = 10 kOe). (e) Field-dependent magnetization at 2 K for Ce3MgBi5. The derivative, dM/dH...

FIG. 3. (a) Temperature dependence of the electrical resistivity ρ(T) of Ce3MgBi5 measured in zero magnetic field from 300 to 1.9 K. The inset shows the temperature derivative dρ/dT, highlighting the antiferromagnetic transition at TN = 4.16 K. (b) Enlarged view of the low-temperature region of ρ(T) in zero field. The red solid line represents a fit to the antiferromagnetic spin-wave scattering model, with ∆fixed to the value obtained from heat-capacity data. The auxiliary curves highlight the relative contributions of the ρ0 + AT 2 and magnon terms. The portions of the curves below 1.8 K are extrapolations (see text). (c) ρ(T) measured at H = 0 and 70 kOe, plotted on a logarithmic...

FIG. 4. (a) Temperature dependence of the specific heat Cp(T) of Ce3MgBi5 and the nonmagnetic reference compound La3MgBi5 measured in zero magnetic field over a wide tem- perature range. The inset highlights the low-temperature re- gion, where a pronounced anomaly at TN = 4.15 K is ob- served for Ce3MgBi5. (b) Low-temperature specific heat of Ce3MgBi5 measured for H ⊥[001] in magnetic fields up to 30 kOe (∆H = 5 kOe). (c) Magnetic contribution to the spe- cific heat plotted as Cmag/T together with the corresponding magnetic entropy Smag(T), obtained by subtracting the phonon background estimated from La3MgBi5. FIG. 5. Magnetic field–temperature phase diagram of Ce3MgBi5 for magnetic fields applied perpendicular to the [001] direction. Dashed lines are guides to the eye.
